name: Cross-audience foundations
when_to_use: Overall documentation system, IA, docs-as-code, style, accessibility, versioning, telemetry

name: Developer docs site
when_to_use: Quickstart, API reference, tutorials, troubleshooting, doc-site IA, search/findability

name: README front door
when_to_use: Front-door README: what-this-is, install on first screen, paste-runnable example, next-step ladder

name: Changelog and release notes
when_to_use: CHANGELOG.md, Keep a Changelog format, breaking-change callouts, release notes

name: Contributor onboarding docs
when_to_use: CONTRIBUTING.md, repo onboarding docs, PR-flow documentation

name: Examples and samples
when_to_use: Runnable sample apps, per-language hello-worlds, snippet freshness

name: End-user help
when_to_use: Help center, in-product help, onboarding, empty states, tooltips, microcopy, user-facing errors

name: API and tool contracts
when_to_use: OpenAPI/MCP/GraphQL descriptions, examples, errors, idempotency, rate limits, operational metadata

name: Audience conflicts
when_to_use: Human-vs-agent or DX-vs-UX documentation tensions where one audience's optimum harms another
